title: DNA Extraction from Ball Python Sheds
author: Jose Avila Cervantes

steps.title: EQUIPMENT Dry Bath / Heated Block...
steps.instruction: EQUIPMENT Dry Bath / Heated Block Microcentrifuge DNA LoBind tubes 1.5mL Micropipettes Assorted pipette tips

steps.title: REAGENTS Lysis Buffer ( 10 millimolar...
steps.instruction: REAGENTS Lysis Buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-base, 100 millimolar (mM) EDTA, 2% SDS, 5 Molarity (M) , NaCl, pH 8 ) TE Buffer (EDTA 1 millimolar (mM) , Tris-Cl 10 millimolar (mM) ) Proteinase K ( 20 mg/mL ) Phenol/Chloroform/Isoamyl Alcohol 25:24:1 (v/v) Ethanol 100 % Ethanol 70 % (Freshly prepared) Tris Acetate-EDTA (TAE) Buffer SYBR Safe DNA stain Agarose Loading Dye Wide Range Ladder (100-12,000 bp)

steps.title: PROTEINASE K DIGESTION 1
steps.instruction: PROTEINASE K DIGESTION 1. Cut a small piece of fresh tissue (3-5mm). Put in a 1,5mL microtube. Add 900 µL of lysis buffer. Add 9 µL Proteinase K ( 20 mg/mL ) and vortex thoroughly for 00:00:30 seconds. 2. Incubate at 55 °C, overnight .

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Phenol/Chloroform/Isoamyl Alcohol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Phenol/Chloroform/Isoamyl Alcohol 25:24:1 (v/v) and vortex thoroughly for 00:00:30 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:05:00 at 16000 x g, °C, .

steps.title: Carefully remove the upper aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the upper aqueous phase (~ 500 µL )and transfer the layer to a fresh tube. Be sure not to carry over any Phenol during pipetting.

steps.title: Add 1000 µL of 100 %...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL of 100 % volume Ethanol, invert the tube and place the tube at -80 °C for 01:00:00 or at -20 °C Overnight .

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 4 °C for 00:30:00 at 16000 x g, °C, to pellet the DNA.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 150 µL of 70 % volume ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL of 70 % volume ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 4 °C for 00:05:00 at 16000 x g, °C, .

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Repeat 70 % volume ethanol wash...
steps.instruction: Repeat 70 % volume ethanol wash once and remove as much of the remaining ethanol as possible.

steps.title: Dry the DNA at Room temperature for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Dry the DNA at Room temperature for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Re-suspend the DNA pellet in 200 µL of TE Buffer
steps.instruction: Re-suspend the DNA pellet in 200 µL of TE Buffer.

steps.title: Prepare a 1% agarose gel with...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1% agarose gel with 1X TAE buffer and 1X SYBR Safe DNA stain.

steps.title: Add 4 µL Wide Range Ladder...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL Wide Range Ladder (100-12,000 bp) in the first well.

steps.title: Premix 9 µL of sample and...
steps.instruction: Premix 9 µL of sample and 1 µL of 10X loading dye or 5 µL of sample and 1 µL of 6X loading dye. Load this mixture in each well.

steps.title: Run the samples for 00:40:00 at a 100V
steps.instruction: Run the samples for 00:40:00 at a 100V.

steps.title: Visualize in a trans illuminator and...
steps.instruction: Visualize in a trans illuminator and take a photo of the gel.
